description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a rounded, bulging midsection and reinforced by three metallic hoops—one at the top, one at the bottom, and one around the center. The wood staves are vertically aligned, showing a natural grain pattern and subtle carved swirls, giving the barrel a rustic and weathered appearance. The metallic hoops are slightly textured and appear to be made of iron or steel, showing some wear and minor dents. The top of the barrel is open, revealing a hollow interior, and the rim is also reinforced with metal. There are no visible handles or spouts, and the surface finish is matte with a slightly rough texture. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, storage areas, or near taverns and markets.
description: A wooden barrel with metal hoops, open at the top, featuring a rustic wood grain and a slightly weathered appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open top, wood grain, and all three metal hoops clearly.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view highlighting the metallic sheen and texture of the hoops, with wood grain visible.
description_view_2: Another angled view emphasizing the curvature of the barrel and the central metal hoop.
description_view_3: Rear view similar to the front, with wood grain and metal hoops visible, no additional features.
